title: tenants — tenancy hooks
description: Per-tenant lifecycle callbacks (#[AsTenancyHook]) that this module registers. Fires on every tenant init and every tenant end for every request. Each hook is symmetric: whatever `onTenantInitialized` mutates, `onTenantEnded` restores — mandatory for Octane safety. See .kiro/steering/tenants-hooks.md.

hooks.onInit: Push `tenant_id`, `tenant_slug`, `application_id` into the current PSR-3 log context. Every log line emitted in the request now carries the tenant + application identifiers.
hooks.onEnd: Restore the previous log context snapshot. Never assume empty — a nested Tenancy::runInTenant() may have already stacked context.

hooks.onInit: Set the cache repository prefix to `tenant:{tenant_id}:` so every cache read/write is namespaced. Prevents accidental cross-tenant cache reads.
hooks.onEnd: Restore the previous prefix (either the application-level prefix or the parent-tenant prefix under nested runInTenant).

priorityBands.100..199: ancillary infra — owned by the `telemetry` module (sentry tag, correlation-id enrichment).
priorityBands.200..299: permission / auth wiring — owned by the `access` module (spatie team scope, guard mapping).
priorityBands.300..399: compliance — owned by the `compliance` module (retention snapshot, consent context).
priorityBands.400+: domain-specific per-tenant warming — owned by feature modules that need it (e.g. `feature-flag` warming a per-tenant cache).

notes: Tenancy OWNS the dispatch but does NOT own the priority list — it publishes the framework-level hooks (LogContext, CachePrefix). Every downstream module contributes its own hooks in its own service provider via #[AsTenancyHook].
